title: On John Dove, Innkeeper, Mauchline.
content:
Here lies Johnny Pidgeon;
        What was his religion?
            Wha e'er desires to ken,
        To some other warl'
        Maun follow the carl,
            For here Johnny Pidgeon had nane!

        Strong ale was ablution,
        Small beer, persecution,
            A dram was memento mori;
        But a full flowing bowl
        Was the saving his soul,
            And port was celestial glory.
meaning:
"On John Dove, Innkeeper, Mauchline." is a quintessential example of Robert Burns's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
